mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment indicates weakening labor demand and impending corporate revenue contraction, causing investors to price in lower earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within three months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and personal savings indicates robust consumer spending power and economic expansion, supporting corporate profit margins and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC, or forward returns turn negative when both proxies rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and boosts corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO sustains values above 103.0, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts signal strong consumer confidence and downstream construction demand, which historically precedes positive equity market performance over the following week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST trends, or the signal generates negative returns in more than 50% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and depressing forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive despite CPI readings consistently above 315.0, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth combined with stable unemployment indicates a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k in a single month
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over PAYEMS alone, or forward returns are negative when both employment rises and unemployment stays below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts coupled with falling real GDP growth signals a contraction in aggregate demand, prompting investors to reduce equity exposure and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite simultaneous drops in housing starts and GDP, or the joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing.
